title: P-ary m-sequence-based c-VEP dataset from Martínez-Cagigal et al. (2023)
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 16 healthy participants performing a code-modulated visual evoked potential (c-VEP) brain-computer interface task using p-ary m-sequences. The study investigates non-binary m-sequence stimulation patterns to enhance user comfort in c-VEP-based BCIs. Data were collected across 5 sessions per subject with 8 runs per session, sampled at 256 Hz from 16 EEG channels, and include 11 stimulus classes with visual presentation events. This is a derivative dataset where each condition from the original single-session recording is stored as a separate session to match the MOABB structure.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired at 256 Hz sampling rate from 16 channels using a standard 1005 montage with 50 Hz line frequency. Participants completed 5 sessions, each containing 8 runs (6 training, 2 testing). The experimental paradigm involved visual presentation of p-ary m-sequence stimuli with 11 distinct classes, with trial intervals of 0-1 second. Although the original dataset was recorded in a single session, each condition is stored as a separate session in this derivative to match the MOABB structure.
